blocks.content: In a blackboard architecture, agents do not message each other directly. Instead, they read from and write to a centralized, shared state space called the 'Blackboard'.

blocks.title: Decoupling Benefits
blocks.content: This decouples agent logic. Agents monitor changes on the blackboard and act only when their specialized inputs are ready.

Question 1: How do agents exchange information in a Blackboard architecture?
- A) Direct network sockets
- B) By posting to and reading from a shared central memory store
- C) Via command-line arguments

Feedback: Option B is correct. The Blackboard is a shared space.
